articles.title: Sabbath Is More than Self-Care
articles.summary: The fruit of the Sabbath isn’t ultimately about our well-being. It’s about having more of God himself.

articles.title: Christians Can Give Two Cheers for AI
articles.summary: Our task isn’t to reject technology or baptize it uncritically but to approach it with hopeful realism.
articles.author: Mitchell G. Bahnsen

articles.title: The Crisis That Broke Lecrae Open and the Belief He Rebuilt From Scratch
articles.summary: Lecrae, who has been open about his faith journey in recent years, shares in his lyrics that he “still believes in the Church, but it’s gon’ take us to put in some work.”

articles.title: Churches Keep Using Romans 14 to Police Women’s Clothing. BibleThinker’s Mike Winger Says They’re Getting It Wrong.
articles.summary: In a live Q&A streamed Jan. 13, Pastor Mike Winger tackled the question of whether or not worship leaders should wear yoga pants—or even certain types of jeans–on stage.
articles.author: Jessica Mouser

articles.title: Jesus Raged? The Righteous Anger of God Incarnate
articles.summary: Have you been caught off guard by the anger of Jesus? There you are, peacefully meditating on the Gospels, or flourishing under a favorite preacher. With great comfort, you’re finding Christ to be master of every situation. He wields concrete images, asks perceptive questions, and seems unfazed b...
articles.author: David Mathis
